01 May 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US Navy Expands AI Mine-Hunting In Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- The United States Navy is ramping up artificial intelligence capabilities to detect naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, according to Reuters.

  • New interceptor missiles successfully tested in the Middle East as Defence Minister visits region UK MOD 01 May 2026 -- The UK military and Gulf partners will be better protected against drone attacks following the successful testing of new British-built Skyhammer interceptor missiles and launchers in Jordan.

  • Trump Claims No Need For Congressional Approval As Hostilities With Iran Have 'Terminated' RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump, in a political battle over his war powers, claimed that a cease-fire announced last month had officially "terminated" hostilities against Iran as part of his argument that he does need congressional approval to continue the conflict.
  • Meeks, Smith, Himes Issue Statement on 60-Day Mark of Iran War House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 01 May 2026 -- Representatives Gregory W. Meeks, Ranking Member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee; Adam Smith, Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee; and Jim Himes, Ranking Member of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence, today issued a joint statement on the 60-day mark of Trump's war in Iran. Under the War Powers Resolution, the president must begin withdrawing U.S. forces if Congress has not authorized hostilities within 60 days.
  • Trump 'Not Satisfied' With 'Messed Up' Iranian Leaders' Proposal RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he is "not satisfied" with a new Iranian proposal delivered by Tehran to Pakistan, which is acting as a mediator in talks with the United States.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Statement on President Trump's Declaration that Iran War is 'Terminated' US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 01 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, issued the below statement after President Trump declared hostilities with Iran 'terminated':
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Statement on 60 Days of President Trump's War with Iran US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 01 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statement marking sixty days since President Trump initiated military conflict with Iran, which under the War Powers Act marks a clear legal threshold that absent congressional authorization, the President must end his use of U.S. forces for hostilities or certify to Congress that he needs up to 30 days to safely remove them:
  • Trump Says Not Satisfied With Latest Iran Proposal on Deal Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Friday that he is not satisfied with the latest proposal from Iran regarding a possible deal.
  • US Imposes New Iran-Related Sanctions on 6 Individuals, 21 Entities, 1 Vessel - Treasury Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- The United States has imposed new Iran-related sanctions on six individuals and 21 entities, the Treasury said on Friday.
  • U.S. Sanctions Tighten Grip on Iran-China Oil Trade US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The United States is taking decisive action to disrupt Iran's illicit oil trade, the primary revenue stream for the Iranian regime to fund terrorism and regional destabilization.
  • Fact Sheet: United States Sanctions Network Facilitating Iran's Illicit Oil Trade US Dept. of State 01 May 2026
  • Economic Fury Targets Iranian Shadow Banking Networks Moving Billions in Foreign Currency US Treasury 01 May 2026 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) is designating three Iranian foreign currency exchange houses and their associated front companies as part of Economic Fury and Treasury's ongoing efforts to disrupt the Iranian regime's financial lifelines that sustain its war effort. Collectively, Iranian exchange houses facilitate billions of dollars in foreign currency transactions each year. Because Iran primarily settles its oil sales in Chinese yuan, these exchange houses play a critical role in converting oil revenues into currencies that are more readily useable by the Iranian military and its partners and proxies.

  • Iran Delivers New Negotiation Plan To Pakistan RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- Iran has delivered a new negotiation proposal to Pakistan, which is acting as mediator in talks with the United States.
  • IRGC Navy vows to enforce Leader's 'historic' directive in Persian Gulf Press TV 01 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC)'s Navy pledges to enforce Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei's recent directive concerning the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz.
  • May 1: Iran's resilience pays off with major strategic gains as US pressure tactics fail Press TV 01 May 2026 -- The Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei, hailed the Iranian nation's emphatic victory on the battlefield and vowed that the same resolve will now defeat America's cultural and economic warfare as well.
  • Iran open to diplomacy if US drops its excessive demands: FM Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Islamic Republic is open to diplomacy in case the United States drops its excessive demands and stops using the language of threats against the country.
  • Leader vows to defeat enemy in economic, cultural wars following Iran's victory on battlefield Press TV 01 May 2026 -- The Leader of the Islamic Revolution has vowed that the Iranian nation will defeat the enemy in the cultural and economic spheres as well, following the victory on the military battlefield.
  • Army Chief vows firm response to any threat against Iran's sovereignty Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Commander-in-Chief of the Iranian Army says the Armed Forces will stand firm in the face of any threat or hostility to defend the country's sovereignty and territorial integrity.
  • Iran presents new proposal to Pakistani mediator: Report Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Iran has reportedly given a new proposal to Pakistan for negotiations with the United States, aiming to put an end to the US-Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran Judiciary chief: US cannot impose will at talks after failing on battlefield Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Iran's Judiciary chief said that the United States cannot impose its demands at the negotiating table after failing to achieve its objectives through military aggression and threats.
  • 'Pentagon is lying': Netanyahu's gamble cost US four times official estimate, Araghchi says Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Pentagon is lying about the financial toll of the war on Iran, noting that the aggression has directly cost the United States $100 billion so far—four times the official estimate.
  • Iran demands reparations from six Arab states for enabling US-Israeli Aggression Press TV 01 May 2026 -- The Iranian ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has renewed the call for holding to account the states participating in the unjustified US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 01 May 2026

  • Germany's vice chancellor blasts Trump's anti-Iran strategy, urges quick end to war of aggression Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Germany's vice chancellor has strongly criticized US President Donald Trump over his anti-Iran strategy and called on him to end the war of aggression against Iran as quickly as possible.
  • UAE Official Rejects Iranian Control Of Strait Of Hormuz RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- UAE diplomatic adviser Anwar Gargash rejected Iranian control over the Strait of Hormuz, advocating for an international, law-based guarantee to ensure maritime security in the post-conflict region.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Ranking Member Shaheen Statement on Reported Changes to U.S. Force Posture in Europe US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 01 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statement on reports that the President plans to reduce U.S. troops levels in Europe:
  • Smith Responds to Trump's Order to Pull 5,000 U.S. Troops from Germany House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 01 May 2026 -- House Armed Services Committee Ranking Member Adam Smith (D-Wash.) released the following statement in response to President Donald Trump's order to remove 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany.

  • Classified Networks AI Agreements DoD 01 May 2026 -- The War Department Announces Agreements with Leading AI Companies to Deploy Capabilities on Classified Networks
  • Trump Says Not Worried About Depletion of US Arms Stocks Amid Iran Conflict Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on Friday dismissed concerns about depleting US weapons stocks due to the armed conflict with Iran.
  • Trump Says Will Probably Consider Pulling Out Some US Troops From Spain, Italy Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said that he will probably consider pulling out some US troops from Spain and Italy.
  • VC-25B Bridge program completes flight testing, prepares for summer rollout US Air Force 01 May 2026 -- The Air Force's VC-25B Bridge aircraft has officially completed modification and flight testing and is being painted. The aircraft is on schedule to roll out in a new red, white and blue livery this summer.
  • U.S. and Canadian Maritime Forces Strengthen Alliance with Atlantic Maritime Security Coalition US Navy 01 May 2026 -- U.S. and Canadian maritime leadership from Commander, U.S. Second Fleet (C2F), Commander, U.S. Coast Guard Atlantic Area, (LANTAREA), Maritime Forces Atlantic & Joint Task Force Atlantic (RCN MCC and JTF-A COM), and Fleet and Maritime Security Canadian Coast Guard (CCG) convened to advance the Atlantic Maritime Security Coalition (AMSC). The coalition, formerly known as the Tri-Party Staff Talks (TPST), brings together key maritime forces to enhance collaboration and ensure interoperability between U.S. and Canadian forces.
  • VMFA-251 pioneers new F-35 communications tactics at WTI USMC News 01 May 2026 -- Marines with Marine Fighter Attack Squadron (VMFA) 251 advanced F-35 and other fixed-wing aircraft ground operations by integrating improved communication and digital interoperability capabilities. They demonstrated these advancements during Weapons and Tactics Instructors course 2-26 at Marine Aviation Weapons and Tactics Squadron One (MAWTS-1), Marine Corps Air Station, Yuma, Arizona, on April 13, 2026.
  • Securing the high ground: US-Australian alliance a decisive advantage in space United States Space Force 01 May 2026 -- For nearly a century, military doctrine was anchored by a fundamental truth: whoever controls the skies controls the battlefield. Today, the air is no longer the ultimate high ground — space is.

  • Pentagon awards weapons contract to drone firm linked to Trump family Press TV 01 May 2026 -- The US Air Force has signed a weapons procurement deal with a company backed by the sons of Donald Trump namely Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr, raising conflict-of-interest concerns in the country's political circels.

  • 24 companies have already joined the private air defense initiative Ukraine MOD 01 May 2026 -- As of April 2026, a total of 24 companies have joined the private air defense initiative.
  • Reform of the Defence Forces of Ukraine: defined service durations and revised service remuneration Ukraine MOD 01 May 2026 -- Following a meeting with Minister of Defence Mykhailo Fedorov, government representatives, and military leadership, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced preparations to launch the reform of the Defence Forces of Ukraine.
  • SSU, SBI and National Police shut down another eight mobilisation evasion schemes Security Service of Ukraine 01 May 2026

  • Military leaders examine the evolution of war Australia DOD 01 May 2026 -- At a recent symposium, military leaders from across the Indo-Pacific and key partner nations examined how the character of war is evolving.
  • Australia, Malaysia reinforce regional security Australia DOD 01 May 2026 -- HMAS Warramunga and KD Lekir have wrapped the Malaysia-Australia Training Exercise (MASTEX) off Australia's west coast, marking another iteration of the long-running bilateral activity.

  • DELIVERY OF PROJECT 17A INDIGENOUS ADVANCED STEALTH FRIGATE 'MAHENDRAGIRI' India PIB 01 May 2026 -- Mahendragiri (Yard 12654), the sixth ship of Nilgiri-class (Project 17A) and fourth ship of the class built at Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDSL), was delivered to the Indian Navy on 30 Apr 2026 at MDSL, Mumbai. The delivery marks a significant milestone in achieving self-reliance in warship design and construction.

  • Senior U.S. General Praises Norway's Defence Buildup Forsvaret - Norwegian Armed Forces 01 May 2026 -- Alexus G. Grynkewich, NATO's Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR), is visiting Norway this week. Eirik Kristoffersen is hosting a three-day program highlighting Norway's strategic role within the Alliance.

  • United Arab Emirates - Advanced Precision Kill Weapons System US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the United Arab Emirates for its purchase of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS) and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $147.6 million.

  • Qatar - Patriot Missile Replenishment US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Qatar for its purchase of PATRIOT Air and Missile Defense replenishment services and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $4.01 billion.
  • Qatar - Advanced Precision Kill Weapons System US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Qatar for its purchase of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS). The estimated total cost is $992.4 million.

  • Kuwait - Integrated Battle Command System US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Kuwait for its purchase of and Integrated Battle Command System and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $2.5 billion.

  • Israel - Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System US Dept. of State 01 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Israel for its purchase of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS) and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $992.4 million.

  • Shaheen, Coons, Ricketts, Colleagues Introduce Bipartisan Resolution Highlighting Chinese Threat to America's Strategic Interests US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 01 May 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Chris Coons (D-DE) and Pete Ricketts (R-NE) led a resolution affirming bipartisan concern over the growing threats China poses to America's national security and economic prosperity at a pivotal moment for U.S.-Chinese relations.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 1 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 01 May 2026
  • Press Conference by Security Council President on Work Programme for May UN Security Council 01 May 2026 -- China's presidency of the Security Council this month will focus on upholding the United Nations Charter, advancing political settlements in the Middle East, supporting stability and development in Africa and strengthening protection of civilians in armed conflict, the country's representative said at a Headquarters press conference today.

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • 'There's not much you can do': Israel confirms Hezbollah drone nightmare in Lebanon Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Israel has admitted its failure to counter Hezbollah's drones, stressing that the aircraft has become one of the most significant challenges facing invading Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

  • Middle East crisis puts aid, food, fuel further out of reach for millions already struggling - UN agencies UN News 01 May 2026 -- As the Middle East crisis continues the humanitarian fallout is worsening, with aid route disruptions and food and fuel price hikes wrecking the lives and the rights of the most vulnerable people worldwide, UN agencies warned on Friday.
  • Israel's discriminatory death penalty law marks grave human rights retrogression, UN anti-racism committee warns OHCHR 01 May 2026 -- Israel's newly adopted "Death Penalty for Terrorists Law" perpetuates racial discrimination against Palestinians and amounts to a grave erosion of human rights, the UN Committee on the Elimination of Racial Discrimination warned today, urging Israel to immediately repeal the law.
  • UNHCR: Middle East crisis ripple effects strain aid efforts beyond region UNHCR 01 May 2026 -- The Middle East crisis has generated far‑reaching ripple effects well beyond the region, with growing consequences for global humanitarian supply chains and the delivery of aid, as spikes in transport costs and freight disruptions are forcing UNHCR, the UN Refugee Agency, to adapt its delivery strategy.

  • Joint Statement by Spain and Brazil on Abduction in International Waters Spain MFA 01 May 2026 -- "The Governments of Spain and Brazil condemn in the strongest possible terms the abduction of two of their citizens in international waters by the Government of Israel."
  • OIC Condemns the Israeli Occupation's Attack on the International Resilience Flotilla to the Gaza Strip Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 01 May 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has condemned in the strongest terms the crime of aggression carried out by the Israeli occupation forces in international waters against the Global Resilience Flotilla, which includes activists from dozens of countries on a relief mission aimed at highlighting and alleviating the unprecedented human suffering in the Gaza Strip, as a result of the illegal Israeli blockade

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Cuba, Venezuela, Ukraine UNOCHA 01 May 2026 -- Gaza sewage pours into displacement sites as sanitation system buckles

  • PLA, China Coast Guard conduct readiness patrols, law enforcement patrols in Huangyan Dao and its surrounding waters Global Times 01 May 2026 -- The naval and air units of the People's Liberation Army (PLA) Southern Theater Command (STC) conducted readiness patrols in the territorial sea and airspace of China's Huangyan Dao and its surrounding areas on Thursday, the Theater Command said in statement on Thursday.

  • AFP assures safe passage for 'Atin Ito' mission to WPS Philippine News Agency 01 May 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) on Friday assured the public that the "Atin Ito" Coalition's fourth civilian mission to Pag-asa Island in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) will have safe passage as the military will be providing maritime domain awareness and coordination.

  • The Situation in Myanmar (Treatment of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi and others) (Statement by Press Secretary KITAMURA Toshihiro) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 May 2026
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Myanmar UN Secretary-General 01 May 2026

  • UNICEF calls for the protection of children, schools and health facilities following deadly attacks in Mali UNICEF 01 May 2026

  • Gang-controlled streets, shuttered newsrooms: How violence is eroding Haiti's media UN News 01 May 2026 -- Journalists working in Haiti are under constant threat of death or injury from rapidly expanding criminal gangs, as they continue to report news and information which they hope will help keep fellow citizens safe.

News Reports

  • Statement by Cuba's Foreign Minister, Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla, Through His X Account Cuba Ministry of Foreign Affairs 01 May 2026 -- "We firmly reject the recent unilateral coercive measures adopted by the #UnitedStates government. These actions demonstrate an intention to impose, once again, collective punishment on the Cuban people."
  • IMPOSING SANCTIONS ON THOSE RESPONSIBLE FOR REPRESSION IN CUBA AND FOR THREATS TO UNITED STATES NATIONAL SECURITY AND FOREIGN POLICY The White House 01 May 2026
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Imposes Sanctions on Cuban Regime Officials Responsible for Repression and Threats to U.S. National Security and Foreign Policy The White House 01 May 2026

  • Russia Will Respond to Attempts to Impose Blockade on Kaliningrad - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- Russia takes seriously statements in NATO and EU countries about a possible blockade of the Kaliningrad Region and will respond decisively to any such attempts, Russian Foreign Ministry Ambassador-at-Large Artyom Bulatov told Sputnik.
  • Russia's Soyuz-5 Rocket Launched From Baikonur Cosmodrome for 1st Time - Roscosmos Sputnik 01 May 2026 -- Russia's Soyuz-5 rocket launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome for the first time, and the test launch proceeded as planned, Russian state space corporation Roscosmos said.

  • KMT pressures lawmakers on defense bill, TPP undecided CNA 01 May 2026 -- Taiwan's main opposition Kuomintang (KMT) on Friday said a special defense budget bill sponsored by its caucus has a "binding political force" on its lawmakers, as the smaller Taiwan People's Party (TPP) deliberates what stance to take on the issue.
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PLA Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 01 May 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 01 May 2026

  • Open Briefing of the United Nations Security Council on North Korea Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 May 2026

  • Max Meizlish: Inside Mojtaba Khamenei's Global Kleptocracy RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- The transition of power in the Islamic republic has long been whispered about in the corridors of Tehran, but the ascension of Mojtaba Khamenei in March as the country's third supreme leader -- following the death of his father, Ali Khamenei, in an air strike -- has fundamentally altered the nature of the Iranian state.
  • Iran categorically rejects UK terror allegations as 'baseless', politically-motivated Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Iran's Embassy in the United Kingdom has categorically dismissed any allegations of involvement in violent incidents on British soil.

  • No: 81, 1 May 2026, Regarding the Nomination of Mr. Ali al-Zaidi to Form the New Government in Iraq Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 01 May 2026
  • Iran welcomes nomination of Iraq's new PM-designate; reaffirms support for Iraqi stability Press TV 01 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has welcomed nomination of Ali Faleh al-Zaidi as Iraq's new prime minister-designate.

  • Secretary of State Sarai concludes successful G7 Development Ministers' Meeting in France Canada 01 May 2026 -- The Honourable Randeep Sarai, Secretary of State (International Development), concluded an official visit to Paris, France, from April 28 to 30, 2026, during which he represented Canada at the G7 Development Ministers' Meeting.
  • Prime Minister Carney speaks with President of Argentina Javier Milei Prime Minister of Canada 01 May 2026 -- Today, the Prime Minister, Mark Carney, spoke with the President of Argentina, Javier Milei.

  • PNG Prime Minister Marape shores up economic partnerships in China Radio Free Asia 01 May 2026 -- Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape advanced his economic agenda while in China this week, but experts stressed that economic benefits from Chinese investment should not come at the expense of the environment or workers' rights.

  • EU Quietly Takes Big Step Toward Montenegro's Accession RFE/RL 01 May 2026 -- EU ambassadors gave a green light to both a 90 billion-euro ($106 billion) loan to Ukraine and the latest round of sanctions on Russia, but another decision went largely unnoticed: the creation of a working group to draft Montenegro's EU accession treaty.

  • World News in Brief: Aung San Suu Kyi released from prison, Israel's new death penalty, Cambodia convictions, healthcare inequalities in South America UN News 01 May 2026 -- Former Myanmar State Counsellor Aung San Suu Kyi has been transferred from prison to house arrest, with her sentence reduced under a prisoner amnesty linked to a Buddhist holiday.
